next_steps: Serum rheumatoid factor, anti-CCP antibodies, ANA, and ANCA testing
Serum complement levels (C3, C4) and cryoglobulin evaluation
Sural nerve and adjacent muscle biopsy to evaluate for vasculitis
Complete laboratory panel for inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP)
Repeat MRI of the cervical and lumbar spine with sedation if necessary to rule out structural compression
Evaluate for underlying malignancy as a potential trigger for vasculitis
Formal rheumatology consultation for evaluation of inflammatory arthropathy
Sural nerve and adjacent muscle biopsy to definitively diagnose vasculitis
Comprehensive autoimmune panel including rheumatoid factor, anti-CCP, ANA, ANCA, C3, C4, and cryoglobulins
Measurement of acute phase reactants (ESR, CRP)
Age-appropriate malignancy screening, specifically CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis
Repeat spinal MRI with sedation to rule out occult structural cord or nerve root pathology
Continued investigation of nutritional status and malabsorption
